mnemonics: OBJ-RETURN-CAR: Objects Returned are Copies, Always Remember (the copy constructor is called)
keyPoints: A function can return an object by value, which creates a copy of the local object.
The copy constructor is automatically invoked when an object is returned by value.
Returning objects by value is essential for method chaining (e.g., obj.method1().method2()).
commonMistakes: Returning a reference or pointer to a local object, which will be destroyed, causing undefined behavior.
Forgetting that returning by value invokes the copy constructor, which can be expensive for large objects.
Not implementing a proper copy constructor when the class manages dynamic memory, leading to shallow copies and double-free errors.
